14.reason: qsort expects const void* parameters; mismatch causes compilation error

42.reason: Out-of-bounds access when i == num-1 (accessing ans[i+1]); loop condition should be i < num - 1. Additionally, this single-pass logic fails to correctly merge more than two consecutive terms with the same exponent.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition should be 'i<num-1' to avoid out-of-bounds access when i=num-1. Also, the logic for combining like terms is insufficient as it only handles two consecutive terms; multiple same exponents may not be fully combined.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Out-of-bounds access when i == num-1 (accessing ans[i+1]); loop condition should be i < num - 1. Additionally, this single-pass logic fails to correctly merge more than two consecutive terms with the same exponent.

51.reason: Should only check ans[i].coe == 0 to skip zero-coefficient terms; checking index is redundant and may incorrectly skip valid constant terms or fail to skip zero-coefficient terms with non-zero exponents.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This condition only skips terms that are exactly (0,0), but zero-coefficient terms from merging may have non-zero exponents (e.g., after cancellation), so they won't be skipped correctly. 31.reason: [The input reading logic is flawed: it assumes every term is followed by a space, but the last term ends with a newline, not a space. This causes the loop to terminate prematurely or read incorrectly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The input reading logic is flawed: it assumes every term is followed by a space, but the last term ends with a newline, not a space. This causes the loop to terminate prematurely or read incorrectly.]

49.reason: [This loop only merges adjacent duplicate exponents once; it fails to handle cases where more than two terms have the same exponent or where merging creates new duplicates later in the array. Also, accessing ans[i+1] when i == num-1 leads to out-of-bounds read.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [This loop only merges adjacent duplicate exponents once; it fails to handle cases where more than two terms have the same exponent or where merging creates new duplicates later in the array. Also, accessing ans[i+1] when i == num-1 leads to out-of-bounds read.]

55.reason: [This output logic incorrectly skips only terms where both coefficient and exponent are zero. However, after merging, some terms may have coefficient zero but non-zero exponent (e.g., due to cancellation like 3x^2 - 3x^2), which should also be omitted. Additionally, terms with zero coefficient but non-zero exponent are printed, which violates the problem requirement of printing only non-zero terms.]
